overview: Converts translatable content of input documents to and from URI escape notation (percent-encoding). Can either decode URI escape sequences back to normal text, or encode characters into `%XX` escape sequences. The set of characters to escape is configurable, and the step operates on filter events in a pipeline.

conversionType.help:
Controls the direction of URI conversion:

- **0** — Un-escape: converts URI escape sequences (e.g. `%20`) back into normal characters.
- **1** — Escape: converts characters into URI percent-encoded escape sequences (e.g. space → `%20`). Which characters get escaped is controlled by the **escapeList** and **updateAll** parameters.

updateAll.help:
Escapes **all non-ASCII characters** (extended characters) to URI escape sequences, in addition to the characters specified in the escape list.

This is useful when the target content must be pure ASCII with all Unicode characters percent-encoded.

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when conversionType is 1 (escape mode)

escapeList.help:
The set of characters that will be percent-encoded when escaping. Each character in the string is treated individually.

The `%` character is **always** escaped regardless of whether it appears in this list.

Two common presets based on [RFC 2396](http://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc2396):

- **All But Marks** — escape everything except RFC 2396 "marks": `-`, `_`, `.`, `!`, `~`, `*`, `'`, `(`, `)`
- **All But Marks And Reserved** — escape everything except marks AND RFC 2396 "reserved" characters: `;`, `/`, `?`, `:`, `@`, `&`, `=`, `+`, `$`, `,`
notes: The `%` character is always escaped even if omitted from the list, to prevent double-encoding.

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when conversionType is 1 (escape mode)

examples: `%{}[]()&` — default set, escapes common URI-special characters
All printable ASCII minus RFC 2396 marks — for maximum encoding safety

processingNotes: The `%` character is always escaped to prevent double-encoding issues, regardless of the escape list contents.
When un-escaping (conversionType=0), the escapeList and updateAll parameters have no effect — all valid `%XX` sequences are decoded.

examples.title: Decode URI-encoded content
examples.description: Un-escape URI sequences in translated content back to readable text.

examples.title: Encode content for URI use
examples.description: Escape special characters in content so it can be safely embedded in URIs.
